modules.label: Seismic Acquisition
modules.description: Classes, properties, relations and instances covering seismic survey acquisition: survey types, sources, receivers, geometry and sampling parameters.
sources: Yilmaz, O. (2001). Seismic Data Analysis. SEG Investigations in Geophysics No. 10.
Sheriff, R.E. & Geldart, L.P. (1995). Exploration Seismology (2nd ed.). Cambridge University Press.

modules.label: Seismic Processing
modules.description: Classes, properties, relations and instances for the seismic processing workflow: noise suppression, deconvolution, velocity analysis, statics, NMO, DMO, migration, stacking and amplitude compensation.
sources: Yilmaz, O. (2001). Seismic Data Analysis. SEG Investigations in Geophysics No. 10.
Sheriff, R.E. & Geldart, L.P. (1995). Exploration Seismology (2nd ed.). Cambridge University Press.

modules.label: Seismic Inversion and Attributes
modules.description: Classes, properties, relations and instances for seismic inversion (post-stack, pre-stack, AVO, FWI), rock properties derived from inversion, seismic attributes, DHI classification, AVO anomaly classes, machine-learning facies interpretation (SOM, PCA) and sweet-spot mapping.
sources: Yilmaz, O. (2001). Seismic Data Analysis. SEG Investigations in Geophysics No. 10.
Russell, B.H. (1988). Introduction to Seismic Inversion Methods. SEG Course Notes.

Chopra, S. & Marfurt, K.J. (2007). Seismic Attributes for Stratigraphic and Structural Interpretation. SEG.
Coleou, T., Poupon, M. & Azbel, K. (2003). Unsupervised seismic facies classification. The Leading Edge, 22(10), 942-953.

SeismicSurvey.name: SeismicSurvey
SeismicSurvey.description: A controlled experiment in which seismic energy is injected into the Earth and the returning wavefield is recorded to image subsurface structure. Encompasses geometry definition, source specification and receiver deployment. Ref: Yilmaz 2001, ch. 1.

LandSurvey.name: LandSurvey
LandSurvey.description: Onshore seismic survey in which sources (vibroseis, dynamite) and receivers (geophones) are deployed on land. Near-surface weathering layer and statics corrections are dominant challenges. Ref: Yilmaz 2001, ch. 3.

MarineSurvey.name: MarineSurvey
MarineSurvey.description: Offshore towed-streamer survey in which a vessel tows one or more hydrophone cables. Dominant configuration for deep-water exploration. Ref: Yilmaz 2001, ch. 1; Sheriff & Geldart 1995.

OBNSurvey.name: OBNSurvey
OBNSurvey.description: Ocean-Bottom Node survey: autonomous 4C (Vx, Vy, Vz + hydrophone) sensor packages placed on the seabed. Enables full-azimuth illumination and longer offsets than towed streamers. Ref: Sheriff & Geldart 1995.

OBSSurvey.name: OBSSurvey
OBSSurvey.description: Ocean-Bottom Seismometer/Cable survey: 4C sensors deployed on cables laid on the sea floor, offering P- and S-wave recording for joint PP/PS inversion.

Source.name: Source
Source.description: Device or process that generates seismic energy and injects it into the ground or water. Characterised by peak frequency, dominant frequency band and energy output. Ref: Yilmaz 2001, ch. 2.

Airgun.name: Airgun
Airgun.description: Marine seismic source that releases compressed air (typically 2000 psi) into the water to generate an impulsive pressure wavelet. Standard deep-water acquisition source. Peak frequency range 10-100 Hz. Ref: Sheriff & Geldart 1995.

Vibroseis.name: Vibroseis
Vibroseis.description: Land seismic source that emits a swept-frequency (chirp) signal via a vibrating baseplate hydraulically coupled to the ground. Allows controlled bandwidth 6-120 Hz. Requires cross-correlation with pilot sweep to produce zero-phase wavelet. Ref: Yilmaz 2001, ch. 2.

Dynamite.name: Dynamite
Dynamite.description: Explosive seismic source detonated in a shallow drill hole to generate a broad-band (5-200 Hz) near-impulsive wavelet. Preferred in areas of complex near-surface topography where vibroseis coupling is poor. Ref: Sheriff & Geldart 1995.

SparkerArray.name: SparkerArray
SparkerArray.description: High-resolution marine source using electrical discharge to vaporise water and generate a broadband (50-1000 Hz) pressure pulse. Used in ultra-high-resolution shallow surveys. Ref: Sheriff & Geldart 1995.

Receiver.name: Receiver
Receiver.description: Sensor that converts mechanical ground or water motion into an electrical signal. Sensitivity, natural frequency and dynamic range determine the recordable bandwidth. Ref: Yilmaz 2001, ch. 2.

Hydrophone.name: Hydrophone
Hydrophone.description: Pressure-sensitive piezoelectric sensor towed in marine streamers. Records scalar pressure wavefield (P-wave only). Sensitivity ~10 mV/Pa. Ref: Sheriff & Geldart 1995.

Geophone.name: Geophone
Geophone.description: Velocity-sensitive electromagnetic sensor planted on the ground surface. Natural frequency typically 10 Hz (land) or 4.5 Hz (OBC). Records particle velocity. Ref: Yilmaz 2001, ch. 2.

AccelerometerMEMS.name: AccelerometerMEMS
AccelerometerMEMS.description: Micro-Electro-Mechanical System accelerometer used in nodal acquisition systems. Flat response from DC to several hundred Hz; measures particle acceleration. Superior low-frequency response compared to geophone. Ref: Sheriff & Geldart 1995.

OBC.name: OBC
OBC.description: Ocean-Bottom Cable: multi-component cable laid on the sea floor carrying collocated hydrophones and geophones (4C). Enables dual-sensor summation and S-wave recording. Ref: Sheriff & Geldart 1995.

AcquisitionGeometry.name: AcquisitionGeometry
AcquisitionGeometry.description: Spatial arrangement of sources and receivers defining illumination, fold, offset distribution and azimuth coverage. Critical for migration operator accuracy. Ref: Yilmaz 2001, ch. 1.

StreamerLayout.name: StreamerLayout
StreamerLayout.description: Marine geometry in which one or more hydrophone cables (streamers) are towed behind a vessel. Characterised by streamer length, cable separation, tow depth and number of streamers.

NodalLayout.name: NodalLayout
NodalLayout.description: Land or OBN geometry employing autonomous wireless nodes deployed in a regular or irregular grid. Enables full-azimuth illumination.

SamplingParameters.name: SamplingParameters
SamplingParameters.description: Temporal and spatial sampling parameters that define the recorded wavefield fidelity and alias constraints. Must satisfy the Nyquist theorem in both time and space. Ref: Yilmaz 2001, ch. 1.

Aliasing.name: Aliasing
Aliasing.description: Artifact arising when the sampling interval violates the Nyquist criterion, causing high-frequency energy to fold back into the recorded spectrum. Temporal aliasing: dt > 1/(2*fmax). Spatial aliasing: dx > v/(2*fmax). Ref: Yilmaz 2001, ch. 1.

ProcessingFlow.name: ProcessingFlow
ProcessingFlow.description: Ordered sequence of processing steps applied to raw seismic data to produce an imaged, interpretable seismic volume. Each step transforms the data in a specific way. Ref: Yilmaz 2001, ch. 1.

NoiseSuppressionFilter.name: NoiseSuppressionFilter
NoiseSuppressionFilter.description: Generic category of operations that attenuate coherent or incoherent noise in seismic data while preserving signal fidelity. Sub-types include DATVF, FXEdit and DenoiseFK. Ref: Yilmaz 2001, ch. 6.

DATVF.name: DATVF
DATVF.name_pt: DATVF — Denoising Adaptativo no Dominio T-V-F
DATVF.description: Data-Adaptive Time-Variant Frequency filter that applies a spatially and temporally varying bandpass to track signal frequency content changes with depth/time, suppressing noise outside the signal band without over-smoothing. Ref: Yilmaz 2001.

FXEdit.name: FXEdit
FXEdit.name_pt: FX Edit — Edicao no Dominio F-X
FXEdit.description: Coherent noise attenuation in the frequency-wavenumber (f-x) domain. Random noise appears as spatially uncorrelated; linear coherent noise is predicted and subtracted via Wiener prediction. Ref: Yilmaz 2001, ch. 6.

DenoiseFK.name: DenoiseFK
DenoiseFK.description: Frequency-wavenumber (f-k) fan filter that attenuates noise with apparent velocity outside the signal fan. Effective for ground-roll (slow, low-frequency) suppression in land data. Ref: Yilmaz 2001, ch. 6.

Deconvolution.name: Deconvolution
Deconvolution.description: Inverse filtering operation that compresses the source wavelet toward a spike (spiking) or reduces its length (predictive), thereby improving temporal resolution and attenuating multiples. Assumes convolutional model: x(t) = w(t)*r(t)+n(t). Ref: Yilmaz 2001, ch. 2.

PredictiveDeconvolution.name: PredictiveDeconvolution
PredictiveDeconvolution.description: Deconvolution in which a prediction distance (lag) greater than one sample is used, concentrating the operation on multiple attenuation at a specified prediction lag (equal to the two-way travel time of the peg-leg multiple). Ref: Yilmaz 2001, ch. 2.

SpikingDeconvolution.name: SpikingDeconvolution
SpikingDeconvolution.description: Wiener least-squares deconvolution with prediction lag=1 sample, aiming to collapse the wavelet to a spike and maximise temporal resolution. Assumes minimum-phase source wavelet and white reflectivity. Ref: Yilmaz 2001, ch. 2.

NMO.name: NMO
NMO.description: Normal Moveout correction: time-shift applied to each trace to remove the hyperbolic offset-dependent arrival time difference and align reflections before stacking. Requires RMS velocity model. Introduces NMO stretch at far offsets. Ref: Yilmaz 2001, ch. 3.

DMO.name: DMO
DMO.description: Dip Moveout correction applied after NMO to remove the dip-dependent component of moveout, transforming the data to zero-offset equivalent before stack. Precedes post-stack migration in conventional processing. Ref: Yilmaz 2001, ch. 4.

Stacking.name: Stacking
Stacking.description: Summation of NMO-corrected CMP traces to produce a single stacked trace with improved signal-to-noise ratio proportional to sqrt(fold). Pre-stack data are retained for AVO and inversion. Ref: Yilmaz 2001, ch. 3.

StaticCorrection.name: StaticCorrection
StaticCorrection.description: Time-shift applied to land seismic traces to compensate for near-surface weathering layer thickness and velocity variations, and for surface topography. Comprises elevation statics (field) and residual statics (processing). Ref: Yilmaz 2001, ch. 3.

AmplitudeCompensation.name: AmplitudeCompensation
AmplitudeCompensation.description: Amplitude restoration to compensate for geometrical spreading loss and anelastic (Q) attenuation with depth, making amplitude proportional to reflectivity for AVO/DHI analysis. Ref: Yilmaz 2001, ch. 1.

SphericalDivergenceCorrection.name: SphericalDivergenceCorrection
SphericalDivergenceCorrection.description: Gain function proportional to t^2 * v^2(t) that compensates for amplitude decay due to spherical divergence of the wavefront. Applied early in the flow before deconvolution. Ref: Yilmaz 2001, ch. 1.

QCompensation.name: QCompensation
QCompensation.description: Inverse-Q filter that restores high-frequency amplitude and phase loss caused by anelastic attenuation (Q factor) during wave propagation. Critical in gas-saturated intervals. Ref: Yilmaz 2001, ch. 7.

Migration.name: Migration
Migration.description: Wave-equation-based imaging operator that collapses diffractions and repositions dipping reflectors to their true subsurface positions. Can be applied pre- or post-stack, in time or depth domain. Ref: Yilmaz 2001, ch. 4-5.

KirchhoffMigration.name: KirchhoffMigration
KirchhoffMigration.description: Asymptotic migration based on the Kirchhoff integral of the scalar wave equation. Computationally efficient for 3D pre-stack time and depth migration; less accurate than RTM in complex velocity structures. Ref: Yilmaz 2001, ch. 4.

RTM.name: RTM
RTM.description: Reverse Time Migration: two-way wave equation migration that propagates the source wavefield forward and receiver wavefield backward in time, applying an imaging condition. Handles complex velocity models (salt bodies) accurately. Computationally intensive. Ref: Yilmaz 2001, ch. 5.

SeismicInversion.name: SeismicInversion
SeismicInversion.description: Process of transforming seismic reflection data into quantitative rock property models (impedance, velocity, density) by solving the forward convolution model in reverse. Requires wavelet estimation and low-frequency model from wells. Ref: Russell 1988.

PostStackInversion.name: PostStackInversion
PostStackInversion.description: Inversion applied to stacked (zero-offset equivalent) seismic data to estimate acoustic impedance Ip = Vp * rho. Most mature and widely applied inversion method. Methods: band-limited, model-based, sparse-spike. Ref: Russell 1988.

PreStackInversion.name: PreStackInversion
PreStackInversion.description: Simultaneous inversion of partial-angle stacks to estimate Vp, Vs and density using the Zoeppritz equations or Aki-Richards linearisation. Enables direct Vp/Vs and elastic impedance extraction. Ref: Russell 1988; Connolly 1999.

AVOInversion.name: AVOInversion
AVOInversion.description: Amplitude Versus Offset inversion that extracts AVO intercept (A) and gradient (B) from CDP gathers using the Shuey two-term approximation, enabling fluid and lithology discrimination. Ref: Yilmaz 2001, ch. 11.

ElasticImpedanceInversion.name: ElasticImpedanceInversion
ElasticImpedanceInversion.description: Inversion of angle-dependent seismic data to Elastic Impedance (EI), the far-offset analogue of acoustic impedance. Defined as EI(theta) = Vp^(1+tan^2θ) * Vs^(-8*K*sin^2θ) * rho^(1-4*K*sin^2θ) where K=Vs^2/Vp^2. Ref: Connolly 1999.

FullWaveformInversion.name: FullWaveformInversion
FullWaveformInversion.name_pt: Inversao de Forma de Onda Completa (FWI)
FullWaveformInversion.description: Iterative inversion that minimises the misfit between observed and modelled waveforms to build high-resolution velocity and density models. Computationally intensive; requires low-frequency data (2-5 Hz). Ref: Yilmaz 2001.

AcousticImpedance.name: AcousticImpedance
AcousticImpedance.description: Product of P-wave velocity and bulk density: Ip = Vp * rho. Primary output of post-stack inversion. Units: g/cm3 * m/s. Used to calibrate well logs and identify lithology/fluid changes. Ref: Russell 1988.

ElasticImpedance.name: ElasticImpedance
ElasticImpedance.description: Angle-dependent impedance generalisation of acoustic impedance for non-zero incidence angle, enabling pre-stack seismic calibration. Defined by Connolly (1999). Units: varies with normalisation convention.

RockProperty.name: RockProperty
RockProperty.description: Quantitative petrophysical or rock-physics parameter derived from seismic inversion or well logs. Includes Vp/Vs ratio, lambda-rho and mu-rho for fluid and lithology discrimination. Ref: Russell 1988.

VpVsRatio.name: VpVsRatio
VpVsRatio.description: Ratio of P-wave to S-wave velocity. Sensitive to fluid saturation: brine-saturated rock Vp/Vs ~1.9-2.1; gas-saturated Vp/Vs ~1.4-1.7 (Gassmann fluid substitution). Derived from PreStackInversion. Ref: Russell 1988.

LambdaRho.name: LambdaRho
LambdaRho.description: Product of Lame parameter lambda and density: lambda*rho. Highly sensitive to pore fluid: gas decreases lambda-rho significantly relative to brine. Part of the lambda-mu-rho (LMR) rock-physics domain. Ref: Russell 1988.

MuRho.name: MuRho
MuRho.description: Product of shear modulus mu and density. Relatively insensitive to pore fluid but sensitive to lithology (shale vs sand). Pair with LambdaRho for fluid-lithology crossplot. Ref: Russell 1988.

SeismicAttribute.name: SeismicAttribute
SeismicAttribute.description: Quantitative measure derived from seismic data through mathematical transformation. Extracts information about structure, stratigraphy, fluid content or lithology not directly visible in the amplitude display. Ref: Chopra & Marfurt 2007.

Coherence.name: Coherence
Coherence.description: Measure of lateral similarity of seismic traces. Low coherence indicates faults, fractures or stratigraphic discontinuities. Original algorithm by Bahorich & Farmer (1995); enhanced by semblance (Marfurt et al. 1998). Ref: Chopra & Marfurt 2007.

Curvature.name: Curvature
Curvature.description: Second spatial derivative of the seismic horizon. Most-positive and most-negative curvature highlight flexures, collapse features and subtle faults. Sensitive to fracture corridors. Ref: Chopra & Marfurt 2007.

RMS.name: RMS
RMS.description: Root Mean Square amplitude computed over a time window. Sensitive to reflectivity strength; often brightens over hydrocarbons (Class III AVO). Ref: Chopra & Marfurt 2007.

SpectralDecomposition.name: SpectralDecomposition
SpectralDecomposition.description: Time-frequency analysis (short-window DFT, CWT or MP) that extracts amplitude and phase at discrete frequencies. Each frequency is sensitive to a specific bed thickness (tuning). Effective for stratigraphic thickness mapping. Ref: Chopra & Marfurt 2007.

InstantaneousPhase.name: InstantaneousPhase
InstantaneousPhase.description: Phase of the analytic signal derived via Hilbert transform. Highlights reflector continuity independent of amplitude strength. Useful for stratigraphic correlation. Ref: Chopra & Marfurt 2007.

Dip.name: Dip
Dip.description: Inline and crossline apparent dip of the seismic event derived from the gradient of the instantaneous phase or coherence scan. Input for dip-steered attribute computation. Ref: Chopra & Marfurt 2007.

Azimuth.name: Azimuth
Azimuth.description: Compass direction of the steepest dip of a reflector, derived jointly with the Dip attribute. Used to detect structural and fracture orientation. Ref: Chopra & Marfurt 2007.

DHI.name: DHI
DHI.description: Direct Hydrocarbon Indicator: seismic anomaly whose character is directly controlled by the presence of gas or oil in the reservoir. Includes bright spots, flat spots, phase reversals and AVO anomalies. Ref: Yilmaz 2001, ch. 11.

AVOAnomaly.name: AVOAnomaly
AVOAnomaly.description: Offset-dependent amplitude anomaly at a reservoir interface classified into four Rutherford-Williams classes (I-IV) based on normal-incidence impedance contrast and gradient sign. Class III (negative intercept, negative gradient) is the classic gas sand anomaly. Ref: Yilmaz 2001, ch. 11.

SOM.name: SOM
SOM.description: Self-Organising Map: unsupervised neural network that projects a multi-attribute seismic volume onto a 2D topology-preserving map for facies classification without prior labels. Ref: Coleou et al. 2003, The Leading Edge.

PCA.name: PCA
PCA.description: Principal Component Analysis applied to a multi-attribute seismic dataset to reduce dimensionality, decorrelate attributes and identify dominant seismic facies components before SOM or supervised classification. Ref: Coleou et al. 2003.

FaciesClassification.name: FaciesClassification
FaciesClassification.description: Assignment of seismic volumes or attribute maps to discrete facies classes that correlate with depositional environment or rock type. Can be supervised (with well labels) or unsupervised (SOM/PCA). Ref: Coleou et al. 2003; Chopra & Marfurt 2007.

SweetSpot.name: SweetSpot
SweetSpot.description: Geospatially bounded zone identified by the intersection of favourable seismic attributes (DHI, AVO anomaly, high porosity inversion) indicating an exploration or production target with elevated hydrocarbon potential. Ref: Chopra & Marfurt 2007.

samplingInterval.name: samplingInterval
samplingInterval.description: Time interval between consecutive recorded samples. Standard values: 1 ms (high-resolution), 2 ms (conventional), 4 ms (low-frequency). Must be dt <= 1/(2*fNyquist). Ref: Yilmaz 2001.

recordLength.name: recordLength
recordLength.description: Total duration of each recorded seismic trace, from shot instant to end of recording. Typical 6-10 s for deep targets.

fold.name: fold
fold.description: Number of independent seismic traces that contribute to a single stacked trace after CMP sorting. Higher fold improves signal-to-noise ratio. Standard 3D: 60-120x. Ref: Yilmaz 2001.

offset.name: offset
offset.description: Distance between source and receiver. Minimum offset determines shallow resolution; maximum offset determines depth penetration and NMO velocity resolution. Must be non-negative. Ref: Yilmaz 2001.

NyquistFrequency.name: NyquistFrequency
NyquistFrequency.description: Maximum alias-free frequency: fN = 1/(2*dt). For dt=2ms, fN=250Hz. Ref: Yilmaz 2001, eq. 1-1.

signalBandwidth.name: signalBandwidth
signalBandwidth.description: Frequency range of meaningful signal energy after instrument and earth filtering. Typical 8-80 Hz for conventional reflection seismics.

peakFrequency.name: peakFrequency
peakFrequency.description: Dominant frequency of the source wavelet or Ricker wavelet. Determines vertical resolution: lambda/4 = v/(4*fPeak). Ref: Yilmaz 2001.

operatingPressure.name: operatingPressure
operatingPressure.description: Working pressure of an airgun array at fire time. Typical value 138 bar (2000 psi). Controls peak-to-bubble ratio.

sweepFrequencyMin.name: sweepFrequencyMin
sweepFrequencyMin.description: Lowest frequency in the vibroseis pilot sweep.

sweepFrequencyMax.name: sweepFrequencyMax
sweepFrequencyMax.description: Highest frequency in the vibroseis pilot sweep.

sweepLength.name: sweepLength
sweepLength.description: Duration of the vibroseis sweep signal in seconds. Longer sweeps improve signal-to-noise ratio after correlation.

streamerLength.name: streamerLength
streamerLength.description: Total active length of a towed hydrophone cable. Controls maximum offset and hence depth penetration.

numberOfStreamers.name: numberOfStreamers
numberOfStreamers.description: Count of towed hydrophone cables. Increases cross-line coverage and survey efficiency.

gridSpacing.name: gridSpacing
gridSpacing.description: In-line and cross-line CMP bin dimensions. Determines lateral spatial aliasing limit: dx <= v/(2*fmax). Typical 12.5x12.5m to 25x25m in 3D surveys.

waterDepth.name: waterDepth
waterDepth.description: Water depth at survey location. Determines ghost frequency, node deployment logistics and cable operation window.

receiverSensitivity.name: receiverSensitivity
receiverSensitivity.description: Transduction efficiency of a receiver. For hydrophones: mV/Pa; for geophones: V/(m/s).

surveyArea.name: surveyArea
surveyArea.description: Total area covered by the seismic survey polygon.

processingStep.name: processingStep
processingStep.description: Ordered step in the processing flow, referencing the class of the operation applied.

stepOrder.name: stepOrder
stepOrder.description: Integer index of the processing step within the flow (1 = first).

operatorLength.name: operatorLength
operatorLength.description: Length of the deconvolution Wiener filter operator.

predictionLag.name: predictionLag
predictionLag.description: Prediction distance in predictive deconvolution, set to the expected peg-leg multiple TWTT.

velocityFieldType.name: velocityFieldType
velocityFieldType.description: Type of velocity model used in NMO or migration: RMS, interval, tomographic.

stretchMute.name: stretchMute
stretchMute.description: Maximum tolerable NMO stretch in percent; traces beyond this limit are muted to prevent low-frequency noise contamination of the stack.

qFactor.name: qFactor
qFactor.description: Quality factor: ratio of elastic energy stored to energy dissipated per cycle. Low Q (20-100) = high attenuation; high Q (200-1000) = low attenuation. Used in Q-compensation filter design.

migrationAlgorithm.name: migrationAlgorithm
migrationAlgorithm.description: Algorithm used in the migration step: Kirchhoff, RTM, FWI, Phase-Shift.

migrationDomain.name: migrationDomain
migrationDomain.description: Whether migration is performed in time or depth domain.

bandpassFilterLow.name: bandpassFilterLow
bandpassFilterLow.description: Low-frequency cut of a bandpass filter applied during processing.

bandpassFilterHigh.name: bandpassFilterHigh
bandpassFilterHigh.description: High-frequency cut of a bandpass filter applied during processing.

gainFunction.name: gainFunction
gainFunction.description: Mathematical form of the amplitude correction applied: t2v2, AGC, balance.

staticShift.name: staticShift
staticShift.description: Time shift in milliseconds applied to a trace by static correction.

acousticImpedance_value.name: acousticImpedance_value
acousticImpedance_value.description: Numerical value of acoustic impedance in g/cm3 * m/s. Typical sand: 6000-10000; shale: 7000-12000; carbonates: 12000-20000.

elasticImpedance_angle.name: elasticImpedance_angle
elasticImpedance_angle.description: Angle of incidence at which the elastic impedance is computed, typically 15, 30 or 45 degrees.

vpVsRatio_value.name: vpVsRatio_value
vpVsRatio_value.description: Numerical value of the Vp/Vs ratio. Gas: 1.4-1.7; brine: 1.9-2.1; carbonates: 1.7-2.0.

lambdaRho_value.name: lambdaRho_value
lambdaRho_value.description: lambda*rho in GPa*g/cm3. Decreases significantly in gas-saturated intervals.

muRho_value.name: muRho_value
muRho_value.description: mu*rho in GPa*g/cm3. Sensitive to rock framework; not to pore fluid.

avoIntercept.name: avoIntercept
avoIntercept.description: Zero-offset reflectivity coefficient A in the Shuey two-term AVO approximation R(theta) = A + B*sin^2(theta). Positive for hard kick, negative for soft kick.

avoGradient.name: avoGradient
avoGradient.description: AVO gradient B in the Shuey two-term approximation. Negative for Class III gas sands (bright spot). Ref: Yilmaz 2001.

avoClass.name: avoClass
avoClass.description: Rutherford-Williams AVO classification: I (+A, -B), II (-A or 0A, -B), IIp (+A, +B), III (-A, -B), IV (-A, +B). Ref: Yilmaz 2001.

coherenceValue.name: coherenceValue
coherenceValue.description: Numerical coherence or similarity value between 0 (discontinuous) and 1 (perfectly coherent).

curvatureType.name: curvatureType
curvatureType.description: Most-positive (k1), most-negative (k2), mean (km) or shape index curvature.

spectralFrequency.name: spectralFrequency
spectralFrequency.description: Frequency component extracted in spectral decomposition, corresponding to a specific tuning thickness.

somNeuronCount.name: somNeuronCount
somNeuronCount.description: Total number of neurons in the SOM grid (rows x cols). Controls classification resolution.

pcaVarianceExplained.name: pcaVarianceExplained
pcaVarianceExplained.description: Cumulative variance explained by the retained principal components, expressed as percentage. Typically 80-95% retained.

inversionMethod.name: inversionMethod
inversionMethod.description: Algorithm used in the inversion: band-limited, model-based, sparse-spike, simultaneous, FWI.

wellCount.name: wellCount
wellCount.description: Number of wells used to constrain the low-frequency model and wavelet extraction in the inversion.

uses_source.name: uses_source
uses_source.description: A SeismicSurvey uses one or more Source devices to generate seismic energy.

deploys_receiver.name: deploys_receiver
deploys_receiver.description: A SeismicSurvey deploys one or more Receiver types.

applies_geometry.name: applies_geometry
applies_geometry.description: A SeismicSurvey is characterised by an AcquisitionGeometry.

respects_nyquist.name: respects_nyquist
respects_nyquist.description: SamplingParameters respects the Nyquist criterion with respect to the signal bandwidth: samplingInterval <= 1/(2*signalBandwidthMax). Violation produces Aliasing.

characterised_by.name: characterised_by
characterised_by.description: A SeismicSurvey is characterised by SamplingParameters.

produces_aliasing.name: produces_aliasing
produces_aliasing.description: Violation of Nyquist constraint produces Aliasing artifact.

is_part_of_survey.name: is_part_of_survey
is_part_of_survey.description: AcquisitionGeometry or SamplingParameters is defined as part of a SeismicSurvey.

collocated_with.name: collocated_with
collocated_with.description: In OBC/OBN deployments a Hydrophone is collocated with a Geophone or AccelerometerMEMS at the same spatial position.

precedes.name: precedes
precedes.description: One processing step temporally precedes another in the processing flow.

part_of_flow.name: part_of_flow
part_of_flow.description: A processing step is part of a ProcessingFlow.

uses_deconvolution.name: uses_deconvolution
uses_deconvolution.description: A ProcessingFlow applies a Deconvolution step.

uses_migration.name: uses_migration
uses_migration.description: A ProcessingFlow applies a Migration algorithm.

compensates_amplitude.name: compensates_amplitude
compensates_amplitude.description: A ProcessingFlow applies an AmplitudeCompensation step.

derived_from.name: derived_from
derived_from.description: A RockProperty or SeismicAttribute is mathematically derived from SeismicInversion output or another attribute.

correlates_with.name: correlates_with
correlates_with.description: A SeismicAttribute correlates with a RockProperty or another SeismicAttribute in a statistical or physical sense.

indicates.name: indicates
indicates.description: A DHI or AVOAnomaly indicates the potential presence of a hydrocarbon accumulation or specific lithofacies.

classified_by.name: classified_by
classified_by.description: A seismic facies volume is classified by FaciesClassification using SOM or PCA.

uses_pca.name: uses_pca
uses_pca.description: A FaciesClassification or SOM uses PCA for dimensionality reduction of seismic attributes.

input_to_inversion.name: input_to_inversion
input_to_inversion.description: Processed seismic data or partial stack is input to a SeismicInversion.

yields.name: yields
yields.description: A SeismicInversion yields a RockProperty volume as output.

combined_in.name: combined_in
combined_in.description: Multiple SeismicAttributes are combined in a FaciesClassification or SweetSpot delineation.

constrained_by_well.name: constrained_by_well
constrained_by_well.description: SeismicInversion is constrained by well log data for low-frequency model and wavelet extraction.

I_BUZ3D_2022.name: Buzios 3D Wide-Azimuth Streamer Survey 2022
I_BUZ3D_2022.description: Broadband wide-azimuth 3D towed-streamer survey over the Buzios carbonate pre-salt field, Santos Basin, offshore Brazil. 14 streamers x 8 km, 100 m cable separation, 6.25 m group interval, 2 ms sample interval.
I_BUZ3D_2022.source: Analogous to Buzios-field 3D acquisition programs described in Petrobras technical literature

I_LAND_VIB_2021.name: Parnaiba Basin Vibroseis Land Survey 2021
I_LAND_VIB_2021.name_pt: Levantamento Terrestre Vibrosseis Bacia do Parnaiba 2021
I_LAND_VIB_2021.description: Orthogonal-template 3D vibroseis survey in the Parnaiba Basin, northeastern Brazil. Four vibrator fleet, 4-8 Hz linear sweep, 16 s sweep length, 2 ms sample interval, maximum offset 6000 m.
I_LAND_VIB_2021.source: Representative of ANP-regulated onshore 3D programs in Brazilian interior basins

I_OBN_LULA_2023.name: Lula OBN 4C Full-Azimuth Survey 2023
I_OBN_LULA_2023.description: Ocean-Bottom Node full-azimuth 4C survey over the Lula giant pre-salt field, Santos Basin. Nodes deployed at 2100 m water depth on 400 x 400 m grid; P- and S-wave data recorded for joint PP/PS inversion and imaging through the salt canopy.
I_OBN_LULA_2023.source: Analogous to Petrobras OBN programs in Santos Basin ultra-deep water

PF_MARINE_RTM.name: Deep-Water Pre-Stack RTM Processing Flow
PF_MARINE_RTM.description: Typical pre-stack depth migration processing flow for deep-water broadband 3D marine data: demultiple, deconvolution, NMO/velocity analysis, SRME, RTM.
PF_MARINE_RTM.source: Representative of Santos Basin pre-salt processing workflows (Yilmaz 2001)

PF_LAND_KIRCHHOFF.name: Land Vibroseis Kirchhoff Time Migration Flow
PF_LAND_KIRCHHOFF.name_pt: Fluxo de Migracao de Kirchhoff em Tempo — Vibrosseis Terrestre
PF_LAND_KIRCHHOFF.description: Standard processing flow for land vibroseis 3D: cross-correlation, statics, bandpass filter, decon, NMO, DMO, Kirchhoff time migration.

PF_OBN_PS.name: OBN 4C PP/PS Joint Processing Flow
PF_OBN_PS.name_pt: Fluxo de Processamento Conjunto PP/PS OBN 4C
PF_OBN_PS.description: Processing flow for OBN 4C ocean-bottom data including dual-sensor summation, P- and S-wave separation, PP/PS registration, NMO for both modes and joint inversion preparation.

attributes.note: PS mode requires Vp/Vs ratio for NMO and registration

INV_BUZ_POSTSTACK.name: Buzios Pre-Salt Post-Stack Acoustic Impedance Inversion
INV_BUZ_POSTSTACK.description: Model-based post-stack inversion of the Buzios 3D seismic volume to acoustic impedance, constrained by 12 wells with full-waveform sonic and density logs. Low-frequency model from well interpolation; wavelet extracted from 10 wells using statistical method.
INV_BUZ_POSTSTACK.source: Representative of pre-salt carbonate inversion programs in Santos Basin; Russell 1988

INV_LULA_PRESTACK.name: Lula Simultaneous Pre-Stack Elastic Inversion
INV_LULA_PRESTACK.description: Simultaneous pre-stack inversion of near, mid and far angle stacks to extract Ip, Is and density, enabling Vp/Vs, lambda-rho and mu-rho volumes for fluid discrimination in the Lula BVE carbonate reservoir.
INV_LULA_PRESTACK.source: Russell 1988; Connolly 1999; representative Santos Basin pre-stack program

ATTR_COHERENCE_BUZ.name: Buzios Coherence Fault/Fracture Volume
ATTR_COHERENCE_BUZ.description: Semblance-based coherence attribute extracted from the Buzios RTM migrated volume to delineate fault and fracture networks in the BVE carbonate reservoir. Computed on a 9-trace moving window with 20ms temporal gate.

INST_AVO_CLASS3.name: Parnaiba Basin Class III AVO Gas Sand Anomaly
INST_AVO_CLASS3.name_pt: Anomalia AVO Classe III Areia Gasosa Bacia do Parnaiba
INST_AVO_CLASS3.description: Class III AVO anomaly at the Devonian Cabecas Formation top: negative intercept (A=-0.08), strong negative gradient (B=-0.14), consistent with gas-saturated low-impedance sand encased in higher-impedance shale. Produces bright spot and phase reversal at the base.
INST_AVO_CLASS3.source: Representative of Devonian gas play in Parnaiba Basin; Yilmaz 2001 AVO classification

meta.label: Geomechanics / MEM Ontology
meta.description: Formal ontology for Mechanical Earth Model (MEM), in situ stress, pore pressure, elastic moduli, rock mechanics properties, failure criteria, and wellbore stability. Covers the four MEM pillars: in situ stress, pore pressure, elastic moduli, and containment geometry.
sources: Fjaer E., Holt R.M., Horsrud P., Raaen A.M., Risnes R. — Petroleum Related Rock Mechanics, 2nd ed. Elsevier 2008 (ISBN 978-0-444-50260-5)
Zoback M.D. — Reservoir Geomechanics, Cambridge University Press 2010 (ISBN 978-0-521-14619-7)
Hoek E., Brown E.T. — The Hoek-Brown Failure Criterion and GSI — 2018 Edition. Journal of Rock Mechanics and Geotechnical Engineering 2019
ISRM — The Complete ISRM Suggested Methods for Rock Characterization, Testing and Monitoring 1974-2006
Anderson E.M. — The Dynamics of Faulting, Oliver & Boyd 1951
Bieniawski Z.T. — Engineering Rock Mass Classifications, Wiley 1989

classes.name: StressTensor
classes.description: Symmetric 3x3 second-rank tensor describing the state of stress at a point in a rock mass. Components sigma_1 (maximum principal), sigma_2 (intermediate principal), sigma_3 (minimum principal). By convention sigma_1 >= sigma_2 >= sigma_3.
classes.description_pt: Tensor simétrico 3x3 que descreve o estado de tensão em um ponto da rocha. Componentes sigma_1 (máxima principal), sigma_2 (intermediária), sigma_3 (mínima). Convenção: sigma_1 >= sigma_2 >= sigma_3.

classes.name: PrincipalStress
classes.description: One of the three principal (normal) stresses acting on planes where shear stress vanishes. In the subsurface: Sv (vertical overburden), SHmax (maximum horizontal), Shmin (minimum horizontal). Anderson regime determines their relative magnitudes.
classes.description_pt: Uma das três tensões principais (normais) agindo em planos onde a tensão de cisalhamento é zero. No subsolo: Sv (sobrecarga vertical), SHmax (horizontal máxima), Shmin (horizontal mínima).

classes.name: ShearStress
classes.description: Stress component acting parallel to a plane. In the Mohr circle representation, shear stress tau = (sigma_1 - sigma_3)/2 * sin(2*theta). Controls shear failure when tau exceeds the Coulomb criterion.
classes.description_pt: Componente de tensão atuando paralelamente a um plano. No círculo de Mohr: tau = (sigma_1 - sigma_3)/2 * sin(2*theta). Controla ruptura cisalhante quando supera o critério de Coulomb.

classes.name: PorePressure
classes.description: Pressure of pore fluid in connected pore space. Controls effective stress via Terzaghi-Biot principle: sigma_eff = sigma_total - alpha * Pp. Calibrated from RFT/MDT measurements, drilling events, seismic velocity inversion (Eaton method).
classes.description_pt: Pressão do fluido no espaço poroso conectado. Controla a tensão efetiva via princípio de Terzaghi-Biot: sigma_eff = sigma_total - alpha * Pp. Calibrada por RFT/MDT, eventos de perfuração, inversão de velocidade sísmica (método Eaton).

classes.name: MudWindow
classes.description: Safe drilling mud weight window bounded below by pore pressure (collapse/kick risk) and above by fracture gradient (lost circulation risk). In high-pressure, high-temperature or depleted reservoirs the window can be very narrow. Distinct from 'janela de geracao' (kerogen maturation window).
classes.description_pt: Janela de peso de lama segura para perfuração. Limite inferior: pressão de poros (risco de colapso/influxo); limite superior: gradiente de fratura (risco de perda de circulação). NOTA: 'Janela de Lama' (M9) NÃO é a mesma coisa que 'Janela de Geração' (M7 geoquímica, faixa de Ro%).
classes.rag_alert: JanelaDeLama (M9 Geomecânica) NÃO é Janela de Geração (M7 Geoquímica). A primeira é intervalo de peso de lama; a segunda é faixa de maturidade Ro%.

classes.name: MohrCircle
classes.description: Graphical representation of the stress state on a plane at a given orientation. Circle diameter equals (sigma_1 - sigma_3); center at (sigma_1 + sigma_3)/2. Failure occurs when the circle touches the failure envelope (Mohr-Coulomb, Hoek-Brown).
classes.description_pt: Representação gráfica do estado de tensão em um plano. Diâmetro igual a (sigma_1 - sigma_3); centro em (sigma_1 + sigma_3)/2. Ruptura ocorre quando o círculo toca o envelope de ruptura.

classes.name: FailureCriterion
classes.description: Mathematical condition defining the stress state at which rock fails. Two principal criteria in petroleum geomechanics: Mohr-Coulomb (linear envelope, suitable for most sedimentary rocks) and Hoek-Brown (curved envelope, better for fractured rock masses).
classes.description_pt: Condição matemática que define o estado de tensão em que a rocha rompe. Dois critérios principais: Mohr-Coulomb (envelope linear, adequado para rochas sedimentares) e Hoek-Brown (envelope curvo, melhor para maciços fraturados).

classes.name: MohrCoulombCriterion
classes.description: Linear shear failure criterion: tau_f = c + sigma_n * tan(phi), where c is cohesion (MPa) and phi is the angle of internal friction (degrees). Parameters derived from triaxial tests. Standard for wellbore stability analysis in sandstones and shales.
classes.description_pt: Critério de ruptura cisalhante linear: tau_f = c + sigma_n * tan(phi), onde c é a coesão (MPa) e phi é o ângulo de atrito interno (graus). Parâmetros obtidos de ensaios triaxiais.

classes.name: HoekBrownCriterion
classes.description: Curved empirical criterion for rock masses: sigma_1 = sigma_3 + sigma_ci * (m_b * sigma_3/sigma_ci + s)^a, where sigma_ci is intact rock UCS, m_b is reduced Hoek-Brown constant, s and a are GSI-dependent parameters. Uses GSI for rock mass characterization. Hoek-Brown 2018 edition is current standard.
classes.description_pt: Critério empírico curvo para maciços rochosos. Usa GSI para caracterizar o grau de fraturamento e resistência do maciço. Edição 2018 de Hoek & Brown é o padrão atual.
sources: Hoek E. et al. Rock Mechanics and Rock Engineering 2002

classes.name: MechanicalEarthModel
classes.description: Integrated quantitative description of the mechanical state of a reservoir and surrounding rock, comprising: (1) in situ stress magnitudes and orientations, (2) pore pressure, (3) elastic and strength properties of rock, (4) structural and stratigraphic geometry. The four-pillar MEM is the foundation for wellbore stability, sand production, hydraulic fracturing, and compaction predictions.
classes.description_pt: Descrição quantitativa integrada do estado mecânico do reservatório e rochas vizinhas. Quatro pilares: (1) tensões in situ, (2) pressão de poros, (3) módulos elásticos e de resistência, (4) geometria estrutural-estratigráfica.

classes.name: MechanicalEarthModel1D
classes.description: One-dimensional MEM built along a wellbore path. Depth functions of pore pressure gradient, fracture gradient, Sv, SHmax, Shmin, UCS, Young's modulus, Poisson's ratio, and Biot coefficient. Output of GEOMECBR software at Petrobras. Calibrated against LOT, MDT, caliper, image logs.
classes.description_pt: MEM unidimensional construído ao longo do poço. Funções de profundidade: gradiente Pp, gradiente de fratura, Sv, SHmax, Shmin, UCS, E, nu, alfa. Output do software GEOMECBR.

classes.name: MechanicalEarthModel3D
classes.description: Three-dimensional MEM integrating seismic, well log, and production data into a volumetric grid. Allows spatial prediction of stress, pore pressure, and rock properties away from wells. Required for field development planning, infill drilling, and stimulation design. Output of GERESIM at Petrobras.
classes.description_pt: MEM tridimensional que integra dados sísmicos, perfilagem e produção em grade volumétrica. Permite predição espacial de tensões e propriedades de rocha. Output do software GERESIM.

classes.name: RockMechProperty
classes.description: Mechanical property of intact rock or rock mass used in geomechanical modelling. Includes elastic properties (Young's modulus, Poisson's ratio, bulk modulus, shear modulus) and strength properties (UCS, tensile strength, cohesion, friction angle). Differentiated as static (laboratory) vs dynamic (sonic log).
classes.description_pt: Propriedade mecânica da rocha intacta ou maciço rochoso usada em modelagem geomecânica. Inclui propriedades elásticas e de resistência. Distinguida como estática (laboratório) vs dinâmica (perfil sônico).

classes.name: UCS
classes.description: Uniaxial Compressive Strength (UCS) — the maximum axial stress a cylindrical rock specimen can sustain under zero confining pressure. Unit: MPa. Typical range: sandstone 10-200 MPa, limestone 30-250 MPa, salt 20-30 MPa. Static UCS (laboratory) differs from dynamic UCS (sonic-derived) by 20-40%; do not substitute without calibration.
classes.description_pt: Resistência à Compressão Uniaxial. Máxima tensão axial que um corpo cilíndrico de rocha suporta com pressão de confinamento zero. Unidade: MPa. UCS estático (laboratório) difere do dinâmico (sônico) em 20-40%.
classes.rag_alert: UCS estático (laboratório) NÃO é igual ao UCS dinâmico (derivado de DTC/DTS via correlação). Diferença típica 20-40%. Não usar dinâmico para projeto sem calibração laboratorial.

classes.name: YoungModulus
classes.description: Young's modulus (E) — ratio of axial stress to axial strain in the elastic regime. Governs reservoir compaction and surface subsidence. Static E from uniaxial/triaxial tests; dynamic E from sonic velocities (Vp, Vs). Unit: GPa. Typical range: 5-90 GPa for sedimentary rocks.
classes.description_pt: Módulo de Young (E) — razão entre tensão axial e deformação axial no regime elástico. Governa compactação do reservatório e subsidência. Estático: ensaio triaxial; dinâmico: velocidades sônicas Vp, Vs.

classes.name: PoissonRatio
classes.description: Poisson's ratio (nu) — ratio of lateral strain to axial strain under axial loading. Dimensionless, physically constrained to [0, 0.5] for isotropic elastic materials (incompressible limit = 0.5). Typical range: 0.1-0.35 for sedimentary rocks. Controls horizontal stress magnitude from vertical load.
classes.description_pt: Razão de Poisson (nu) — razão entre deformação lateral e axial sob carregamento axial. Adimensional, fisicamente limitado a [0, 0.5] para materiais elásticos isotrópicos. Controla magnitude das tensões horizontais.

classes.name: BiotCoefficient
classes.description: Biot coefficient (alpha) — poroelastic coupling parameter relating pore pressure to total stress in Biot's effective stress principle: sigma_eff = sigma_total - alpha * Pp. Range: 0 (undeformable skeleton) to 1 (unconsolidated sediment). Defined as alpha = 1 - K_dry / K_grain.
classes.description_pt: Coeficiente de Biot (alfa) — parâmetro de acoplamento poroelástico. Tensão efetiva = tensão total - alfa * Pp. Varia de 0 (esqueleto rígido) a 1 (sedimento não consolidado). alfa = 1 - K_dry / K_grain.
sources: Biot M.A. Journal of Applied Physics 1941

classes.name: BulkModulus
classes.description: Bulk modulus (K) — resistance of rock to volumetric compression. K = E / (3*(1 - 2*nu)). Used in compaction modelling and fluid substitution (Gassmann equations). Unit: GPa.
classes.description_pt: Módulo volumétrico (K) — resistência da rocha à compressão volumétrica. K = E / (3*(1 - 2*nu)). Usado em modelagem de compactação e substituição de fluido (equações de Gassmann).

classes.name: ShearModulus
classes.description: Shear modulus (G) — resistance of rock to shear deformation. G = E / (2*(1 + nu)). Directly measured from shear sonic velocity (Vs): G = rho * Vs^2. Unit: GPa. Unaffected by pore fluid (insensitive to fluid type in Gassmann).
classes.description_pt: Módulo de cisalhamento (G) — resistência da rocha à deformação cisalhante. G = E / (2*(1 + nu)). Medido pela velocidade sônica de cisalhamento Vs: G = rho * Vs^2. Insensível ao fluido nos poros (Gassmann).

classes.name: RockMassRating
classes.description: Bieniawski (1989) RMR system classifying rock masses on a scale 0-100 based on six parameters: UCS, RQD, spacing, condition of discontinuities, groundwater, and orientation adjustment. RMR I (81-100): very good; RMR V (0-20): very poor rock mass.
classes.description_pt: Sistema RMR de Bieniawski (1989) que classifica maciços rochosos de 0 a 100 com base em seis parâmetros. RMR I (81-100): muito boa; RMR V (0-20): rocha muito ruim.
sources: Bieniawski Z.T. Engineering Rock Mass Classifications 1989

classes.name: GSI
classes.description: Geological Strength Index (Hoek 1994) — quantifies rock mass quality for use in the Hoek-Brown criterion. Range 0-100. GSI = RMR_1989 - 5 (approximate). High GSI (>70): massive, blocky rock; low GSI (<30): heavily sheared, tectonically disrupted.
classes.description_pt: Geological Strength Index (Hoek 1994) — quantifica a qualidade do maciço rochoso para o critério Hoek-Brown. Varia de 0 a 100. GSI alto (>70): rocha maciça/em blocos; baixo (<30): maciço muito fraturado.

classes.name: RQD
classes.description: Rock Quality Designation (Deere 1963) — percentage of core pieces longer than 100 mm in total core run. Ranges 0-100%. RQD > 90%: excellent; 75-90%: good; 50-75%: fair; 25-50%: poor; < 25%: very poor. Input to RMR, Q-system, and GSI.
classes.description_pt: Rock Quality Designation (Deere 1963) — porcentagem de pedaços de testemunho com mais de 100 mm no comprimento total. 0-100%. Entrada para RMR, sistema Q e GSI.

classes.name: Anisotropy
classes.description: Directional dependence of mechanical properties in a rock mass. Transverse isotropy (VTI) is common in shales (Thomsen parameters epsilon, delta, gamma). Anisotropy of elastic moduli, strength (UCS varies with bedding orientation), and permeability (kH/kV). Critical for pre-salt carbonate geomechanics.
classes.description_pt: Dependência direcional das propriedades mecânicas da rocha. Isotropia transversal (VTI) é comum em folhelhos (parâmetros de Thomsen). Anisotropia de módulos elásticos, resistência (UCS varia com orientação do acamamento) e permeabilidade (kH/kV).

classes.name: Brittleness
classes.description: Tendency of rock to fail suddenly without significant plastic deformation. High brittleness favors natural fracturing and hydraulic fracture complexity. Quantified by brittleness index B = (sigma_c - sigma_t)/(sigma_c + sigma_t) or by (E_norm + nu_norm)/2 (Rickman 2008). Key predictor for unconventional reservoir stimulation.
classes.description_pt: Tendência da rocha a romper abruptamente sem deformação plástica significativa. Alta fragilidade favorece fraturamento natural e complexidade de fraturas hidráulicas. Índice de fragilidade B ou (E_norm + nu_norm)/2 (Rickman 2008).

classes.name: AndersonStressRegime
classes.description: Anderson (1951) classification of tectonic stress regimes based on the relative magnitudes of Sv, SHmax, Shmin. Normal faulting (NF): Sv > SHmax > Shmin; Strike-slip (SS): SHmax > Sv > Shmin; Reverse faulting (RF): SHmax > Shmin > Sv. Controls natural fault type and drilling hazards.
classes.description_pt: Classificação de Anderson (1951) dos regimes tectônicos baseada nas magnitudes relativas de Sv, SHmax, Shmin. Falha normal (NF): Sv > SHmax > Shmin; Transcorrente (SS): SHmax > Sv > Shmin; Falha reversa (RF): SHmax > Shmin > Sv.

classes.name: Wellbore
classes.description: Cylindrical cavity in rock mass whose stability is governed by the stress concentration around the opening. Near-wellbore stress concentration factor depends on Shmin/SHmax ratio and wellbore orientation. Source of caliper, image log, and LOT/XLOT data used to calibrate the MEM.
classes.description_pt: Cavidade cilíndrica na rocha cuja estabilidade é governada pela concentração de tensões ao redor da abertura. Origem de dados de caliper, imagem e LOT/XLOT usados para calibrar o MEM.

classes.name: LaudoGeomecanico
classes.description: Technical document that consolidates the geomechanical analysis associated with a well location or well, bringing together geological, structural, seismic and operational context, as well as geomechanical risks, geopressure curves/models and stress information when available. Serves as formal technical evidence (role_type: formal_technical_evidence) to support well design and monitoring. Produced by CSD Geomecânica (RES/GMR or responsible geomechanics area). Note: distinct from the QPG (geological prognosis) and from purely post-drilling monitoring reports — the Laudo can span pre-drilling prognosis through monitoring phases.
classes.description_pt: Documento técnico que consolida a análise geomecânica associada a uma locação ou poço, reunindo informações de contexto geológico, estrutural, sísmico e operacional, além de riscos geomecânicos, curvas/modelos de geopressões e tensões quando disponíveis. Serve como evidência técnica formal (role_type: formal_technical_evidence) para subsidiar o projeto e o acompanhamento do poço. Elaborado pelo CSD Geomecânica (RES/GMR ou área responsável). Nota: distinto do QPG (prognóstico geológico) e de relatórios exclusivamente de monitoramento pós-perfuração — o Laudo pode abranger tanto a fase de prognóstico pré-perfuração quanto o acompanhamento.

primary_use: Consolidar evidências técnicas de geopressões, tensões e ocorrências

known_content_sections.description: Informações básicas do poço ou locação.

known_content_sections.section_name: Localização em mapa e seção sísmica
known_content_sections.description: Contextualização espacial e sísmica da locação.
Interpretação do contexto estrutural relevante para análise de geohazards.
Descrição por unidades ou intervalos, quando aplicável.
Seção dedicada à avaliação dos riscos geomecânicos identificados ao longo da trajetória.
Curvas de pressão de poros, gradientes e demais limites operacionais quando disponíveis.
Representação do modelo geomecânico usado ou atualizado para análise.
Registro ou referência a eventos geomecânicos relevantes.

official_datastore.name: VGE
official_datastore.role: repositório observado para documentos de monitoramento geomecânico e relatórios associados

official_datastore.name: Pasta de poço / drive científico
official_datastore.role: localização observada para Relatório de Monitoramento Geomecânico

official_datastore.name: SIPLEX
official_datastore.role: candidato para armazenamento/consulta de documentos de locação

quality_rules.rule: Não tratar Laudo Geomecânico e QPG como a mesma entidade.
Não inferir existência de Laudo Geomecânico para todos os poços.
Quando o documento for Relatório de Monitoramento Geomecânico CSD, classificar como documento relacionado, mas não necessariamente como Laudo pré-perfuração.
Separar evidências pré-perfuração, tempo real e post-mortem.

graph_rag_behavior.disambiguation_rule: Se a consulta mencionar laudo, relatório geomecânico, geohazards, riscos geomecânicos, locação, projeto de poço, curva de geopressão ou monitoramento geomecânico, recuperar GEOMEC026B antes de documentos genéricos.
must_return_with: QPG quando a pergunta envolver previsão geológica
Ocorrências geomecânicas quando a pergunta envolver eventos observados
Curvas de geopressões quando a pergunta envolver janela operacional
Modelo geomecânico quando a pergunta envolver tensões ou geohazards
example_queries: Existe Laudo Geomecânico para este poço?
Quais riscos geomecânicos foram apontados no laudo?
O laudo contém curvas de geopressões?
Quais ocorrências foram consideradas na análise?
Qual foi o subsídio do laudo para o projeto do poço?
Compare o QPG com o Laudo Geomecânico.

evidence_gaps: Confirmar fonte canônica normativa para a definição formal do Laudo Geomecânico, especialmente se o padrão oficial é PE-2RES-00009.
Confirmar owner_department definitivo entre RES/GMR, Ativo de Produção, EXP/OGP/AQG ou outro órgão responsável.
Confirmar datastore oficial definitivo para Laudo pré-perfuração versus Relatório de Monitoramento Geomecânico pós-perfuração.
Confirmar se Relatório Geomecânico de Poço e Laudo Geomecânico devem ser entidades separadas ou subtipos.
Confirmar quais campos são obrigatórios para ingestão: poço, locação, campo, bacia, data, versão, responsável técnico, modelo usado, curvas anexas e referências.

properties.name: youngModulus
properties.description: Young's modulus E in GPa. Static from triaxial test; dynamic from Vp/Vs. Relationship E_dyn = f(Vp, Vs, rho).

properties.name: ucs
properties.description: Uniaxial Compressive Strength in MPa. ISRM: very low <25 MPa, low 25-50, medium 50-100, high 100-250, very high >250.

properties.name: poissonRatio
properties.description: Poisson's ratio nu. Physical constraint: 0 <= nu <= 0.5 for isotropic elastic materials.

properties.name: porePressureGradient
properties.description: Pore pressure gradient. Normal = 0.433 psi/ft (freshwater) to 0.465 psi/ft (seawater brine). Overpressure > 0.6 psi/ft. Calibrated from RFT/MDT, kick margin, Eaton sonic method.

properties.name: biotCoefficient
properties.description: Biot coefficient alpha. alpha = 1 - K_dry/K_grain. Measured in laboratory by comparing drained and undrained bulk moduli.

properties.name: bulkModulus
properties.description: Bulk modulus K in GPa. K = E / (3*(1 - 2*nu)).

properties.name: shearModulus
properties.description: Shear modulus G in GPa. G = E / (2*(1 + nu)). Also G = rho * Vs^2.

properties.name: sigma1
properties.description: Maximum principal stress sigma_1 in MPa. sigma_1 >= sigma_2 >= sigma_3.

properties.name: sigma2
properties.description: Intermediate principal stress sigma_2 in MPa. sigma_1 >= sigma_2 >= sigma_3.

properties.name: sigma3
properties.description: Minimum principal stress sigma_3 in MPa. sigma_1 >= sigma_2 >= sigma_3. Equals Shmin in normal faulting regime.

properties.name: sv
properties.description: Vertical stress Sv (overburden) in MPa. Calculated by integrating density log: Sv = integral(rho_bulk * g * dz). Gradient typically 1.0-1.1 psi/ft.

properties.name: sHmax
properties.description: Maximum horizontal stress SHmax in MPa. Magnitude constrained by frictional strength of faults. Orientation determined from borehole breakouts and drilling-induced tensile fractures.

properties.name: shmin
properties.description: Minimum horizontal stress Shmin in MPa. Most reliably constrained by XLOT/mini-frac closure pressure. Controls fracture gradient and hydraulic fracture propagation direction.

properties.name: cohesion
properties.description: Cohesion c in MPa (Mohr-Coulomb parameter). Intercept of the failure envelope on the shear stress axis. Zero for unconsolidated sands; high for cemented sandstones and carbonates.

properties.name: frictionAngle
properties.description: Angle of internal friction phi in degrees (Mohr-Coulomb). Slope of the failure envelope: tan(phi). Typical range 20-40 deg for sedimentary rocks.

properties.name: rmrScore
properties.description: Bieniawski RMR89 score. Sum of six parameter ratings. Classes: I (81-100) very good; II (61-80) good; III (41-60) fair; IV (21-40) poor; V (0-20) very poor.

properties.name: gsiValue
properties.description: GSI value (Geological Strength Index) 0-100. Approximate: GSI = RMR89 - 5.

properties.name: rqdValue
properties.description: RQD value in percent. Pieces > 100 mm / total core run length * 100%.

properties.name: brittlenessIndex
properties.description: Brittleness index B. Multiple formulations exist. Rickman (2008) SPE 115258: B = (E_norm + (1 - nu_norm))/2 where E_norm and nu_norm are normalized to [0,1] within the formation range.

properties.name: mudWeightLower
properties.description: Lower bound of mud weight window in g/cm3 (equivalent to ppg * 0.12). Set at or above pore pressure gradient to prevent influx.

properties.name: mudWeightUpper
properties.description: Upper bound of mud weight window in g/cm3. Set at or below fracture gradient to prevent lost circulation.

properties.name: stressRegime
properties.description: Anderson stress regime classification. normal_faulting: Sv > SHmax > Shmin; strike_slip: SHmax > Sv > Shmin; reverse_faulting: SHmax > Shmin > Sv.

relations.name: induces_failure
relations.description: Stress state induces failure in a rock mass when it exceeds the failure criterion. Domain: StressTensor; Range: FailureCriterion.

relations.name: controls_mud_window
relations.description: Pore pressure and fracture gradient jointly control the mud weight window boundaries.

relations.name: triggers_sand_production
relations.description: Critical drawdown condition where near-wellbore stress exceeds rock strength triggers sand production (sanding). Predicted by comparing effective wellbore stress against UCS.

relations.name: affects_wellbore_stability
relations.description: MEM predicts wellbore stability: collapse pressure (lower bound) and fracture pressure (upper bound) at each depth in the well.

relations.name: caused_by
relations.description: The width of the mud window is caused_by (influenced by) the stress regime; narrow windows occur in compressive regimes.

relations.name: derivative_with_respect_to
relations.description: Relation between elastic properties, e.g., Young's modulus and Poisson's ratio are derivative_with_respect_to the sonic velocities Vp and Vs.

relations.name: bounds_drilling_window
relations.description: Pore pressure gradient bounds the drilling window from below; fracture gradient bounds it from above.

relations.name: calibrated_by
relations.description: 1D MEM is calibrated_by wellbore observations: LOT/XLOT data, caliper breakouts, image logs (FMI/UBI), MDT/RFT measurements.

relations.name: constrained_by
relations.description: Stress magnitudes are constrained_by frictional limits on pre-existing faults (Mohr-Coulomb); the in situ stress cannot exceed the critically stressed state.

relations.name: derived_from_sonic
relations.description: Dynamic elastic moduli (E_dyn, G_dyn, K_dyn, nu_dyn) are derived_from_sonic velocity logs (DTC/DTS). Require static-dynamic conversion for engineering use.

relations.name: input_to_mem
relations.description: Rock mechanical properties (UCS, E, nu, Biot) serve as input_to_mem and are integrated into the four pillars of the Mechanical Earth Model.

relations.name: governs_fracture_propagation
relations.description: The minimum principal stress (Shmin) governs_fracture_propagation direction and determines the fracture gradient (upper bound of mud window).

instances.label: Buzios Field 1D MEM — Conceptual Example
instances.label_pt: MEM 1D Campo de Búzios — Exemplo Conceitual
instances.description: Illustrative 1D MEM for a pre-salt carbonate well in the Santos Basin, offshore Brazil. Reflects published ranges for Barra Velha/Itapema carbonates. Values are illustrative, not proprietary data.

mud_window_ppg.note: Very narrow window in carbonate pre-salt interval

instances.note: Illustrative values only. Derived from published literature on Santos Basin pre-salt geomechanics. No proprietary Petrobras data.

instances.label: Mohr-Coulomb Failure — Sandstone Example
instances.description: Example application of Mohr-Coulomb to a typical shallow-marine sandstone. Cohesion and friction angle are within published ranges for weakly cemented sands.

instances.label: Hoek-Brown — Fractured Carbonate Example
instances.description: Hoek-Brown criterion applied to a fractured carbonate reservoir with moderate GSI. GSI reflects blocky, interlocked rock with slightly weathered joint surfaces.
sources: Hoek et al. Rock Mechanics and Rock Engineering 2002

instances.label: Normal Faulting Regime — Santos Basin
instances.label_pt: Regime de Falha Normal — Bacia de Santos
instances.description: Typical extensional tectonic setting of the Santos Basin passive margin. Vertical stress (overburden) is greatest principal stress. Controls dip-slip normal faults and wellbore breakout azimuth.
sources: Chang C. et al. Mar. Petrol. Geol. 2010

instances.label: Narrow Mud Window — Depleted Reservoir Example
instances.label_pt: Janela de Lama Estreita — Reservatório Depletado
instances.description: A depleted reservoir (Pp reduced by 20-30% of initial pressure) causes Shmin to decrease proportionally, narrowing or closing the safe drilling window. Example of a field with pore pressure depletion causing drilling difficulty in infill wells.

mud_window_ppg.note: Window narrows from 3.3 ppg to 0.7 ppg after depletion

instances.label: Rock Mass Classification Chain — GSI from RQD and RMR
instances.label_pt: Cadeia de Classificação de Maciço — GSI a partir de RQD e RMR
instances.description: Demonstration of the classification chain: RQD -> RMR -> GSI -> Hoek-Brown parameters. Example for a moderately fractured basement rock.

meta.label: Fracture Ontology Branch — Geomechanics
meta.description: Fracture and deformation structure ontology branch, extending SWEET deformation and linking to GSO structural geology classes (gso-faults, gso-folds). Covers fracture types, fracture networks, fault zone anatomy, and Anderson stress regime controls on fracturing.
sources: Fjaer E., Holt R.M., Horsrud P., Raaen A.M., Risnes R. — Petroleum Related Rock Mechanics, 2nd ed. Elsevier 2008
Zoback M.D. — Reservoir Geomechanics, Cambridge University Press 2010
Twiss R.J., Moores E.M. — Structural Geology, 2nd ed. W.H. Freeman 2007
Caine J.S., Evans J.P., Forster C.B. — Fault zone architecture and permeability structure. Geology 1996
Fossen H. — Structural Geology, 2nd ed. Cambridge University Press 2016
Brodaric B., Richard S. — Geoscience Ontology (GSO) v1.0.2. GSC Open File 8796, DOI 10.4095/328296, 2021

classes.name: DeformationMechanism
classes.description: Process by which rock changes shape or volume. Mechanisms include brittle fracturing, plastic flow, pressure solution, and cataclastic flow. Controls fracture type and permeability evolution.
classes.description_pt: Processo pelo qual a rocha muda de forma ou volume. Mecanismos incluem fraturamento frágil, fluxo plástico, solução por pressão e fluxo cataclástico.

classes.name: BrittleDeformation
classes.description: Deformation by fracturing and faulting at low temperature and pressure (shallow crustal conditions). Produces joints, faults, and shear bands. Dominant deformation style in most reservoir settings.
classes.description_pt: Deformação por fraturamento e falhamento em baixas temperaturas e pressões (condições crustais rasas). Produz juntas, falhas e bandas de cisalhamento.

classes.name: DuctileDeformation
classes.description: Deformation by crystal plasticity, pressure solution, or viscous flow at elevated temperature and pressure. Produces mylonite zones, foliation, and boudinage. Occurs in deep reservoirs and at higher metamorphic grades.
classes.description_pt: Deformação por plasticidade cristalina, solução por pressão ou fluxo viscoso em altas temperaturas e pressões. Produz zonas de milonito, foliação e boudinage.

classes.name: FractureType
classes.description: Classification of fractures by genesis and kinematics: joint (opening mode I), fault (shear mode II/III), vein (mineralized opening fracture), and shear band (deformation band in porous media).
classes.description_pt: Classificação de fraturas por gênese e cinemática: junta (modo I de abertura), falha (modo II/III de cisalhamento), veio (fratura aberta mineralizada), banda de cisalhamento.

classes.name: Joint
classes.description: Opening-mode fracture (Mode I) with negligible shear displacement. Aperture driven by tensile effective stress exceeding tensile strength. Two sub-types: extension joints (sigma_3 orthogonal to fracture) and hybrid joints. Common in tight carbonates and shales.
classes.description_pt: Fratura em modo de abertura (Modo I) com deslocamento cisalhante negligível. Abertura controlada pela tensão efetiva de tração. Comum em carbonatos compactos e folhelhos.

classes.name: Fault
classes.description: Shear-mode fracture with significant displacement. Classified by slip kinematics: normal (dip-slip, extensional), reverse (dip-slip, compressional), strike-slip (lateral movement). Controls hydrocarbon migration pathways and trap integrity.
classes.description_pt: Fratura em modo de cisalhamento com deslocamento significativo. Classificada por cinemática: normal (extensional), reversa (compressional), transcorrente (lateral).

classes.name: Vein
classes.description: Mineralized fracture filled with crystalline material (calcite, quartz, dolomite). Records paleo-fluid flow directions and trapping conditions. Aperture measurements on core and image logs indicate former open fractures.
classes.description_pt: Fratura mineralizada preenchida com material cristalino (calcita, quartzo, dolomita). Registra direções de fluxo de paleofluidos. Medidas de abertura em testemunho e imagem indicam fraturas abertas pretéritas.

classes.name: ShearBand
classes.description: Tabular zone of localized shear strain in porous rock without a discrete fracture surface. Associated with grain crushing (cataclasis) and porosity/permeability reduction. Important in weak, high-porosity sandstones and chalk.
classes.description_pt: Zona tabular de deformação cisalhante localizada em rocha porosa sem superfície de fratura discreta. Associada ao esmagamento de grãos e redução de porosidade/permeabilidade.

classes.name: FractureSet
classes.description: Group of sub-parallel fractures with similar orientation, type, and inferred origin. Characterized by mean orientation (dip, dip direction), aperture distribution, and spacing. Statistical description is foundation of Discrete Fracture Network (DFN) modelling.
classes.description_pt: Grupo de fraturas sub-paralelas com orientação, tipo e origem similares. Caracterizado por orientação média (mergulho, azimute), distribuição de abertura e espaçamento. Base do modelamento DFN.

classes.name: FractureNetwork
classes.description: Interconnected system of fracture sets and/or faults forming a hydraulically connected network. Connectivity determines effective permeability tensor. Modelled as Discrete Fracture Network (DFN) or as equivalent porous medium. Critical for pre-salt carbonate reservoir flow modelling.
classes.description_pt: Sistema interconectado de conjuntos de fraturas formando uma rede hidraulicamente conectada. Conectividade determina tensor de permeabilidade efetiva. Modelada como DFN ou meio poroso equivalente.

classes.name: Stylolite
classes.description: Irregular dissolution surface in carbonates formed by pressure solution along grain contacts under compressive stress. Acts as permeability barrier orthogonal to sigma_1. Identified in core and image logs as dark, irregular, sutured surfaces. Oriented perpendicular to the maximum compressive stress.
classes.description_pt: Superfície irregular de dissolução em carbonatos formada por solução por pressão. Atua como barreira de permeabilidade. Identificada em testemunho e imagens como superfícies escuras, irregulares e suturadas. Orientada perpendicularmente a sigma_1.

classes.name: FaultZone
classes.description: Three-component architectural model of a mature fault (Caine et al. 1996): (1) fault core — principal slip surfaces and gouge; (2) damage zone — subsidiary fractures and folds flanking the fault; (3) host rock — undeformed protolith. Permeability structure controls fluid flow.
classes.description_pt: Modelo arquitetural de três componentes de uma falha matura (Caine et al. 1996): (1) núcleo da falha — superfícies de deslizamento e gouge; (2) zona de dano — fraturas subsidiárias; (3) rocha hospedeira — protólito não deformado.

classes.name: FaultCore
classes.description: Central zone of a fault containing principal slip surfaces, fault gouge (clay-rich pulverized rock), cataclasite, and/or mylonite. Typically has very low porosity and permeability (clay smear). Shale Gouge Ratio (SGR) is key predictor of sealing capacity.
classes.description_pt: Zona central da falha contendo superfícies de deslizamento, gouge de falha (rocha pulverizada rica em argila), cataclasito e/ou milonito. Baixíssima porosidade e permeabilidade. SGR é preditor-chave da capacidade selante.

classes.name: DamageZone
classes.description: Region flanking the fault core characterized by elevated fracture density, increased permeability, and subsidiary faults and folds. Width scales with fault displacement (D): width ~ 10^0.3D (m). Enhanced permeability makes damage zones important conduits for fluid flow.
classes.description_pt: Região flanqueando o núcleo da falha com densidade de fraturas elevada, maior permeabilidade e falhas/dobras subsidiárias. Largura proporcional ao deslocamento da falha. Permeabilidade elevada torna zonas de dano condutos importantes de fluido.

classes.name: NormalFault
classes.description: Dip-slip fault where the hanging wall moves downward relative to the footwall. Formed in extensional tectonic settings (Sv > SHmax > Shmin). Typical dip 50-70 degrees. Creates grabens and half-grabens. Dominant fault type on passive continental margins.
classes.description_pt: Falha de dip-slip onde o teto desce em relação ao piso. Formada em regime extensional (Sv > SHmax > Shmin). Mergulho típico 50-70 graus. Cria grabens e meio-grabens. Tipo dominante em margens continentais passivas.

classes.name: ReverseFault
classes.description: Dip-slip fault where the hanging wall moves upward relative to the footwall. Formed in compressional settings (SHmax > Shmin > Sv). Low angle (<30 deg) = thrust fault; high angle (>30 deg) = reverse fault. Creates fold-thrust belts and compressional traps.
classes.description_pt: Falha de dip-slip onde o teto sobe em relação ao piso. Formada em regime compressional (SHmax > Shmin > Sv). Ângulo baixo (<30 graus) = falha de empurrão; alto ângulo = falha reversa.

classes.name: StrikeSlipFault
classes.description: Fault with predominantly lateral (strike-parallel) displacement. Formed in strike-slip (wrench) tectonic settings (SHmax > Sv > Shmin). Sub-vertical fault planes. Creates pull-apart basins (releasing bends) and push-up ridges (restraining bends). Transform faults are a special case.
classes.description_pt: Falha com deslocamento predominantemente lateral. Formada em regime transcorrente (SHmax > Sv > Shmin). Planos sub-verticais. Cria bacias pull-apart e altos estruturais.

properties.name: fractureAperture
properties.description: Fracture aperture (opening width) in mm. Controls hydraulic transmissivity (cubic law: T ~ aperture^3). Natural fractures: 0.01-10 mm. Mechanical aperture (geometric) vs hydraulic aperture (flow-equivalent).

properties.name: fractureSpacing
properties.description: Mean spacing between adjacent fractures in a set, measured perpendicular to fracture planes. Determines fracture density and bulk permeability enhancement.

properties.name: fractureStrikeDeg
properties.description: Azimuth of the fracture strike (0-360 degrees from North). Determined from oriented core, image logs (FMI/UBI), or seismic discontinuity attributes.

properties.name: fractureDipDeg
properties.description: Dip angle of the fracture plane (0-90 degrees from horizontal). Vertical joints dip 90 deg; horizontal fractures dip 0 deg.

properties.name: sgrValue
properties.description: Shale Gouge Ratio (SGR) in percent. SGR = sum(shale_thickness * fraction_shale) / fault_displacement * 100. SGR > 18-20% = potentially sealing; < 18% = potentially leaking. Computed in TrapTester (Badley Geoscience).
sources: Yielding G. et al. Petroleum Geoscience 1997
properties.rag_alert: SGR (Shale Gouge Ratio) NÃO é SAR (saturados/aromáticos/resinas) nem SGR de gás. É um proxy geomecânico de selância de falha.

properties.name: faultDisplacementM
properties.description: Cumulative displacement along fault slip surface in meters. Scales with fault length (D ~ L / 20 to L / 100) and damage zone width.

properties.name: fractureDensityPerMeter
properties.description: Number of fractures per unit length (P10 or P32 measure). P10 = fractures per meter of scanline. Higher density increases effective permeability.

meta.description: Crosswalk between Geolytics Fracture Ontology classes and GSO/Loop3D Geoscience Ontology structural geology classes. Follows the same SKOS-based mapping pattern as data/osdu-gso-crosswalk.json.
sources: Brodaric B., Richard S. — Geoscience Ontology (GSO) v1.0.2. GSC Open File 8796, DOI 10.4095/328296, 2021
Fjaer E. et al. — Petroleum Related Rock Mechanics, 2nd ed. Elsevier 2008
Fossen H. — Structural Geology, 2nd ed. Cambridge University Press 2016
meta.license_note: Use respects CC BY 4.0 of GSO; cite Brodaric & Richard 2021, GSC OF 8796, DOI 10.4095/328296.

match_kinds.closeMatch: Similar but with scope or definition nuances — use with caution in cross-queries.
match_kinds.broadMatch: Geolytics fracture class is more specific than GSO class.
match_kinds.narrowMatch: Geolytics fracture class is more general than GSO class.

mappings.note: Both define Fault as a discrete surface or zone with shear displacement separating two rock masses. The Geolytics definition adds geomechanical context (Anderson regimes, stress controls) not present in GSO.

Both represent the broader zone of deformation surrounding a fault slip surface. Geolytics adds the Caine et al. (1996) three-component architecture (core, damage zone, host rock) not explicitly in GSO.
Kinematic definition is identical: hanging wall descends relative to footwall in extensional setting. Geolytics adds Anderson stress regime condition (Sv > SHmax > Shmin).

Kinematic definition identical: hanging wall ascends in compressional setting. Geolytics distinguishes thrust (< 30 deg) from reverse fault (> 30 deg) as sub-types, adds Anderson condition (SHmax > Shmin > Sv).
Both define lateral (strike-parallel) dominant displacement. Geolytics adds Anderson condition (SHmax > Sv > Shmin) and sub-types (sinistral, dextral) implied but not enumerated here.
GSO Fracture is the generic class for any brittle discontinuity; Geolytics Joint is specifically an opening-mode (Mode I) fracture. Joint is a sub-type of Fracture.

GSO Deformation is broader (any change in shape or position); Geolytics DeformationMechanism focuses on the physical process (brittle fracturing, plastic flow, pressure solution). Use closeMatch — do not substitute 1:1.
GSO JointSet is restricted to joints (Mode I); Geolytics FractureSet encompasses any sub-parallel group of fractures regardless of mode (joints, shear fractures). Broader scope in Geolytics.
